description:
Current post-worker orchestration is still not truly orchestrating validation failures. When delegated work fails the configured validation gates, the parent session merely reports the failure and then background supervision reruns the same failing validation on an interval until a human enters the worktree and fixes it manually.

Scope:
- stop treating validation failure as a passive poll/retry loop
- when the session is idle and delegated work fails validation, drive a real remediation attempt in the worker worktree instead of just rerunning the same commands forever
- ensure the orchestrator reaches a truthful terminal state when remediation is not possible, instead of repeated noisy retries
- add regression coverage for the remediation / stop-retrying behavior so dogfood runs no longer require manual rescue by default

comments.text: Implemented the first real remediation path in commit `e3e37da`. When delegated worker validation fails, the orchestrator now launches one automatic remediation pass back into the same worktree instead of immediately falling into `attention`, and if that remediation attempt still does not yield a passing validation result the worker is left in a truthful exhausted-attention state rather than blindly rerunning the same failing checks forever. Added persistence/diagnostics fields plus regression coverage for both remediation launch and no-blind-retry behavior.

title: Make validation failures trigger real remediation instead of blind retries
